_comment: NASA TEMPO (Tropospheric Emissions: Monitoring of Pollution) is a geostationary satellite launched April 2023 measuring hourly NO2 + O3 + HCHO over North America at ~2-10 km resolution. Values below model NYC tropospheric NO2 column densities at typical late-morning conditions: industrial corridors 10-15 × 10^15 molec/cm², urban core 7-10, residential 4-7, water/parks 2-4. Anchored on TEMPO L2 NO2 retrievals from clear-sky NYC days in 2024. Convert column → surface NO2 ppb via the boundary-layer mixing-height heuristic (column / mixing_height ≈ ppb), then NO2 ppb → AQI per EPA breakpoints.
based_on: NASA TEMPO L2 NO2 daily product, modeled on clear-sky NYC observations 2024
